title: Cabinet Nightstand with Base Moulding
summary: A face-framed nightstand cabinet with a single door, plywood side panels, and mitered base moulding for a built-in look.
description:
A proper little cabinet nightstand: a face-framed box with recessed plywood side panels, a single hinged door, and a wrap of 5-1/4" base moulding around the bottom that lifts it from 'plywood box' to 'furniture.' It's a rewarding intermediate build — you get face-frame joinery, a fitted door, and moulding mitres without committing to a full dresser.

Build the box from the frames out. Make the two side frames and the front/back frames, and before you assemble the box, drill upward-facing 3/4" pocket holes on the inside top edges so you can attach the top from below later. The plywood side panels tuck inside the frames and get pinned with short (3/4") finish nails so the points don't blow through the 1/4" panel.

The door isn't in the shopping list on purpose — door styles vary — but the rule is fixed: measure your actual finished opening, then build the door 1/4" smaller so you land a 1/8" gap on every side. Hang it on inset wrap hinges or surface-mount hinges. Miter the base moulding at the corners, and add cove moulding under the top and around the panels if you want to dress it up further.

materials.name: Cabinet hinges (inset wrap or surface mount)
Cove moulding
materials.note: Optional dress-up under the top and around panels.

cutList.note: Edge-band the exposed edges if desired.
Build 1/4" less than your measured opening for a 1/8" gap all around.

steps.title: Build the side frames
steps.body: Build the two side frames from 1x2 stiles and 1x3 rails, pocket-holed together. These become the recessed-panel sides of the cabinet.

steps.title: Build the front and back frames and assemble the box
steps.body: Build the front and back frames from 1x3. Before assembling the box, drill 3/4" pocket holes facing upward on the inside top of all the sides for attaching the top later. Then join the side frames to the front and back into the cabinet box.

steps.title: Add the bottom
steps.body: Add the 3/4" plywood bottom to the frames, using 3/4" pocket holes around all sides of the bottom shelf to lock it in.

steps.title: Install the side panels
steps.body: Attach the 1/4" plywood side panels to the inside of the side frames with finish nails and glue. Use short (3/4") nails so the points don't punch through the thin panel.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top panel with even overhangs on the sides and front — leave room at the back for the back panel coming next. Fasten with 1-1/4" pocket screws and glue through the upward-facing pocket holes.

steps.title: Add the back
steps.body: Attach the 1/4" back with glue and finish nails. It squares the cabinet.

steps.title: Wrap the base moulding
steps.body: Cut and attach the base moulding around the bottom with glue and finish nails, mitering the corners. Add cove moulding under the top and around the panels now if you want the dressed-up version.

steps.title: Build and hang the door
steps.body: Measure and confirm your actual opening, then build the door 1/4" less than the opening so it hangs with a 1/8" gap all around. Install with inset wrap hinges or surface-mount hinges. Sand and finish the whole cabinet.
materials: Cabinet hinges (inset wrap or surface mount)

images.alt: Single-door cabinet nightstand with recessed side panels and base moulding
